message:
Add TUI layout module and improve supervision signature handling

- Extract layout logic into dedicated module for reusability
- Fix signature parsing edge cases in supervision
- Update beads issue tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"process_triage-2ka","title":"EPIC: Phase 7 - UX Refinement and Premium TUI","description":"## Overview
Implement the premium TUI experience described in section 7 of the plan. This is the user-facing polish that makes pt feel like an 'alien artifact' rather than a script.

## Core Requirements (from Plan Section 7)

### 7.0 Golden Path (One Coherent Run)
- Default `pt` behavior is a single coherent workflow: scan → infer → plan → TUI confirmation → staged execution → after-action summary
- Everything else available as subcommands/flags for experts
- Every run has a durable session_id and artifact directory

### 7.1 Evidence Ledger
- Show top 3 Bayes factors and residual evidence per process
- Posterior, confidence, evidence contributions

### 7.2-7.3 Confidence and Explainability
- Evidence glyphs for I/O, CPU, TTY, orphan, wchan, net
- 'Why' summary: top evidence items and weights
- 'What would change your mind' (VOI hint)

### 7.4 Full-Auto Approval Flow (TUI + Robot Mode)
- Single 'Apply Plan' TUI screen with pre-toggled recommendations
- Drill-down evidence, bulk toggles
- `--robot` bypasses TUI (still honors --shadow and --dry-run)

### 7.5 Premium TUI Layout (Stripe-level in a Terminal)
- Plan-first UI (not list-first): top 'Plan Summary' bar
- Persistent system bar: load/memory/pressure + overhead budget
- Two-pane layout: left = candidates table, right = drilldown (why, ledger, process tree, impact)
- Bottom action bar: Apply Plan, Export Bundle, Render Report, Toggle View, Help
- Progressive disclosure: one-line 'why' by default, full ledger on demand
- Visual language: KEEP/PAUSE/THROTTLE/KILL action badges, SAFE/CAUTION/DANGER risk badges

### 7.6 Interaction Design (Keyboard-First)
- One-keystroke actions: / search, f filter, s sort, enter details, space toggle, a apply, e export, r report, g galaxy-brain, ? help
- Bulk operations with confirmations
- After-action diff showing before/after snapshot and outcomes
- Micro-interactions: staged progress, smooth transitions, inline sparklines

### 7.7 Sharing & Reporting UX
- One command/keybinding to export .ptb bundle and render HTML report
- Reports look like incident dashboards

### 7.8 Galaxy-Brain Mode (Math Transparency + Fun)
- Keybinding 'g' toggles full math view in detail pane
- Shows: posterior by class, posterior odds vs thresholds, expected-loss table, top Bayes factors, per-feature log-likelihood contributions, FDR/alpha-investing state, VOI
- Math cards with equations + substituted numbers + output:
  - Posterior core
  - Time-varying hazard
  - Conformal interval (runtime/CPU)
  - Conformal class set
  - e-values / e-FDR
  - Alpha-investing
  - VOI
- CLI flag: --galaxy-brain or --explain full
- Report tab with same ledger (equations + numbers, respecting redaction)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default `pt` runs the golden path workflow
- [ ] TUI has two-pane layout with drilldown
- [ ] Galaxy-brain mode toggle works
- [ ] Evidence glyphs and badges are consistent
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts are implemented
- [ ] After-action diff is displayed
- [ ] Export and report are one-keystroke accessible

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Phase 4 (inference), Phase 5 (decision theory), Phase 6 (action execution)
- Blocks: Phase 16 (documentation)

## Technical Notes
- Use a TUI library like ratatui (Rust) for premium layout
- Galaxy-brain math rendering may use terminal-based LaTeX or ASCII equations
- System bar should update in real-time during scans","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:49:51.640093101Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:49:51.640093101Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-2ka","depends_on_id":"process_triage-2ka.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:15.104996883Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-2ka","depends_on_id":"process_triage-h89","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:05:19.270312120Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-2ka","depends_on_id":"process_triage-sj6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:09:16.988505628Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-2ka.1","title":"EPIC: Premium TUI with Modern Ratatui Best Practices","description":"## Overview
Enhance the Premium TUI implementation (Phase 7) with modern ratatui best practices from 2025/2026.

## Background
Ratatui has evolved significantly with v0.30.0+ introducing a modular workspace architecture. The current beads reference ratatui but miss modern patterns and third-party widgets that would accelerate development and improve UX.

## Research Findings (January 2026)

### Ratatui v0.30.0+ Modular Workspace
- Main crate re-exports everything for application convenience
- Widget library authors should depend on ratatui-core for stability
- Improved compilation times and API stability

### Third-Party Widget Ecosystem (from awesome-ratatui)
1. **rat-widget**: Data-input (text-input, date/number-input, text-area, checkbox, radiobutton, slider, calendar), structural widgets (view, split, tabbed, multi-page), table for large datasets, file-dialog, menubar+sub-menus, status-bar. Built-in crossterm event-handling and focus-handling.
2. **ratatui-code-editor**: Code editor widget with syntax highlighting via tree-sitter (useful for config editing)
3. **ratatui-image**: Image widget supporting sixels and unicode-halfblocks (useful for screenshots, charts)
4. **edtui**: Vim-inspired editor widget (natural for developer audience)
5. **tui-react/tui-realm**: React-like paradigm for complex state management

### Architecture Recommendations
1. **Immediate rendering with intermediate buffers**: Each frame renders all widgets
2. **Alternate Screen + Raw Mode**: Standard terminal handling pattern
3. **Constraint-based layouts**: Percentage/fixed/min/max constraints for responsive design
4. **Block widget**: Framing and borders as composition pattern

## Scope Additions
1. Adopt rat-widget for data-input components (config editing, search)
2. Implement responsive constraint-based layouts
3. Add syntax highlighting for config/JSON views via ratatui-code-editor patterns
4. Consider vim-style navigation (edtui patterns) for power users
5. Add image support for richer evidence visualization (if terminal supports sixels)
6. Implement React-like state management pattern for complex views

## Implementation Guidance
- Use cargo-generate template: \`cargo generate ratatui/templates\` for initial structure
- Follow three beginner tutorials from ratatui.rs for team onboarding
- Reference \`demos/\` in ratatui repo for widget patterns
- Unit test TUI components per ratatui testing guide

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/src/tui/widgets_test.rs\`
- **Coverage target**: 85% line coverage for widget logic
- Test cases:
  - Widget rendering produces expected buffer content
  - Constraint calculations correct for various terminal sizes
  - Focus handling works across widget hierarchy
  - Keyboard event routing to correct handlers
  - State transitions in React-like model

### Integration Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/tui_integration.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Full TUI app lifecycle (init → render → event → shutdown)
  - Theme switching preserves layout
  - Responsive behavior at edge terminal sizes (80x24, 200x60)
  - Graceful degradation without Unicode support

### E2E Tests
- **File**: \`test/tui_e2e.bats\`
- Test scenarios:
  - Launch TUI, navigate to process detail, exit cleanly
  - Filter processes using search widget
  - Execute plan via TUI with confirmation
  - Export evidence ledger to file via TUI
- **Artifact logging**: Record terminal output via script/asciinema for debugging

### Visual Regression Tests
- Snapshot-based testing for key screens
- Golden file comparison using \`insta\` crate
- Test matrix: light theme, dark theme, high-contrast

---

## Logging Specifications

### Structured Log Events
| Event | Level | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------|---------|
| \`tui.startup\` | INFO | terminal_size, color_support, unicode | TUI initialization |
| \`tui.render_frame\` | TRACE | frame_number, duration_ms | Performance tracking |
| \`tui.user_input\` | DEBUG | key, modifiers, widget_focus | Input debugging |
| \`tui.widget_error\` | WARN | widget_id, error_message | Widget failures |
| \`tui.shutdown\` | INFO | reason, dirty_state | Clean exit tracking |

### Log Levels by Component
- **Widget rendering**: TRACE (very verbose)
- **State management**: DEBUG
- **User actions**: INFO
- **Errors and recovery**: WARN/ERROR

### Telemetry Integration
- Emit render latency metrics to telemetry lake
- Track widget usage patterns for UX improvement
- Session recording (opt-in) for issue reproduction

---

## Error Handling

### Failure Modes
| Scenario | Detection | Recovery | User Message |
|----------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| Terminal too small | size < 80x24 | Show minimal view or exit | "Terminal too small (need 80x24, have WxH)" |
| Unicode not supported | capability detection | ASCII fallback mode | "Using ASCII mode (Unicode unavailable)" |
| Widget panic | catch_unwind wrapper | Restore terminal, log panic | "TUI error occurred - see logs" |
| Event loop hang | watchdog timer (5s) | Force render cycle | (Internal recovery, no user message) |

### Graceful Degradation
1. No mouse → keyboard-only mode (fully functional)
2. No 256-color → 16-color palette
3. No Unicode → ASCII borders and indicators
4. No alternate screen → inline rendering with clear

---

## Performance Targets
- Frame render: < 16ms (60fps capable)
- Input latency: < 50ms from keypress to visual feedback
- Memory overhead: < 10MB for TUI vs CLI mode
- Startup time: < 200ms to first render

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TUI uses ratatui v0.30.0+ modular patterns
- [ ] rat-widget integrated for form inputs
- [ ] Layouts adapt to terminal size (responsive)
- [ ] Vim-style keybindings optional toggle
- [ ] Code/config syntax highlighting in detail panes
- [ ] State management follows React-like pattern
- [ ] All unit tests pass with 85%+ coverage
- [ ] E2E test suite passes in CI
- [ ] Logging meets specification above
- [ ] Performance targets met on reference hardware

## Dependencies
- Parent: process_triage-2ka (Phase 7 - UX Refinement)
- Depends on: Testing infrastructure (process_triage-aii)

 {"id":"process_triage-2ka.1.1","title":"Implement rat-widget integration for TUI forms","description":"## Overview
Integrate rat-widget for data-input components in the TUI, enabling rich form inputs for configuration editing and search.

## Background
rat-widget provides production-ready TUI widgets including text input, number input, date input, checkboxes, radio buttons, tables, and more. This replaces custom widget implementations with battle-tested components.

## Scope

### 1. Widget Integration
- Add rat-widget dependency to pt-core
- Integrate focus handling with existing TUI state
- Map rat-widget themes to pt color scheme
- Handle keyboard events through rat-widget's event system

### 2. Target Widgets
- **TextInput**: For search, filter expressions
- **NumberInput**: For threshold configuration
- **Checkbox/Radio**: For multi-select, option groups
- **Table**: For process list (with sorting, filtering)
- **Select**: For classification filter dropdown
- **Slider**: For confidence threshold adjustment

### 3. Configuration Editing
- Edit priors.json via TUI
- Edit policy.json via TUI
- Validation feedback in-widget
- Save/cancel with confirmation

### 4. Search Enhancement
- Live filtering as you type
- Regex support in search
- Fuzzy matching option
- Search history (last 10)

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/src/tui/widgets/rat_integration_test.rs\`
- **Coverage target**: 85% for widget integration
- Test cases:
  - TextInput captures input correctly
  - NumberInput validates ranges
  - Checkbox state toggles correctly
  - Table sorting works for all columns
  - Focus navigation follows expected order
  - Theme mapping produces correct styles

### Integration Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/rat_widget_integration.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Full config edit flow: load → edit → validate → save
  - Search filter reduces displayed items correctly
  - Widget state persists across renders
  - Error display for invalid input

### E2E Tests
- **File**: \`test/tui_widgets_e2e.bats\`
- Test scenarios (using pseudo-terminal):
  - Type in search, verify filtered results
  - Navigate to config, edit value, save
  - Use slider to adjust threshold
  - Verify changes persisted to disk
- **Artifact logging**: Input sequences, final state

### Visual Tests
- Snapshot testing for widget appearance
- Golden files for each widget in various states
- Compare across terminal sizes

---

## Logging Specifications

### Structured Log Events
| Event | Level | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------|---------|
| \`widget.init\` | DEBUG | widget_type, id | Widget created |
| \`widget.focus\` | TRACE | widget_id, from_widget | Focus changed |
| \`widget.input\` | TRACE | widget_id, input_type | Input received |
| \`widget.validate\` | DEBUG | widget_id, valid, error | Validation result |
| \`widget.save\` | INFO | widget_id, field | Value saved |

---

## Error Handling

### Failure Modes
| Scenario | Recovery | User Message |
|----------|----------|--------------|
| Invalid input | Show error, keep focus | "Invalid value: REASON" (in-widget) |
| Save failed | Show error, offer retry | "Could not save: REASON. [Retry] [Cancel]" |
| Widget panic | Catch, restore UI | "Widget error. Press any key to continue." |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] rat-widget integrated in cargo dependencies
- [ ] TextInput works for search
- [ ] Table widget displays process list
- [ ] Config editing via TUI works
- [ ] Validation feedback shown in widgets
- [ ] Focus navigation is intuitive
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 85%+ coverage
- [ ] Visual tests pass

## Dependencies

-{"id":"process_triage-2ka.1.2","title":"Implement responsive constraint-based TUI layouts","description":"## Overview
Implement responsive constraint-based TUI layouts that adapt to terminal size using ratatui's constraint system.

## Background
Different terminal sizes require different layouts. Constraint-based layouts (percentage, min/max, ratio) allow the TUI to adapt gracefully from 80x24 to 300x80.

## Scope

### 1. Constraint System
- Use ratatui Constraint types: Percentage, Min, Max, Ratio, Length
- Define layout rules for each view
- Test at standard sizes: 80x24, 120x40, 200x60
- Graceful degradation for tiny terminals

### 2. Layout Definitions

**Process List View:**
- Sidebar: Min(20), Max(40), 20% of width
- Main content: Fill remaining
- Status bar: Length(1) at bottom

**Evidence Detail View:**
- Process info: 30% height
- Evidence ledger: 50% height  
- Actions: 20% height

**Galaxy Brain View:**
- Math display: 60% width
- Explanation: 40% width
- Scrollable within constraints

### 3. Breakpoints
- **Large** (>= 120 cols): Full layout, all panels
- **Medium** (80-119 cols): Collapsed sidebar, stacked panels
- **Small** (< 80 cols): Single panel, navigation menu

### 4. Dynamic Resizing
- Handle SIGWINCH for terminal resize
- Smooth transition between layouts
- No flicker on resize
- Preserve scroll position on resize

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/src/tui/layout_test.rs\`
- **Coverage target**: 90% for layout calculation
- Test cases:
  - Constraint calculation at various sizes
  - Breakpoint detection correct
  - Layout switch at boundaries
  - Min/Max constraints enforced
  - Ratio calculations accurate

### Integration Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/layout_integration.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Full render at 80x24 produces valid output
  - Full render at 200x60 produces valid output
  - Resize event triggers relayout
  - Content fits within calculated areas

### E2E Tests
- **File**: \`test/tui_layout_e2e.bats\`
- Test scenarios:
  - Start TUI at 80x24, verify small layout
  - Resize to 120x40, verify medium layout
  - Resize to 200x60, verify large layout
  - Content visible at all sizes
- **Artifact logging**: Layout dimensions at each breakpoint

### Visual Regression Tests
- Screenshot at each breakpoint
- Golden file comparison
- Detect layout regressions

---

## Logging Specifications

### Structured Log Events
| Event | Level | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------|---------|
| \`layout.calculate\` | TRACE | width, height, breakpoint | Layout calculated |
| \`layout.resize\` | DEBUG | old_size, new_size, new_breakpoint | Terminal resized |
| \`layout.breakpoint_change\` | INFO | from, to | Breakpoint changed |
| \`layout.constraint_applied\` | TRACE | area, constraint_type, result | Constraint resolved |

---

## Error Handling

### Failure Modes
| Scenario | Recovery | User Message |
|----------|----------|--------------|
| Terminal too small | Show minimal view | "Terminal too small. Resize for full view." |
| Layout calculation panic | Use fallback | (Internal recovery, no message) |
| Resize during render | Queue re-render | (No visible impact) |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Layouts defined for all views
- [ ] Three breakpoints (small/medium/large) work
- [ ] SIGWINCH handling works
- [ ] No flicker on resize
- [ ] Content fits at all sizes
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 90%+ coverage
- [ ] Visual regression tests pass

## Dependencies
- Part of: Premium TUI epic (process_triage-2ka.1)

 {"id":"process_triage-2kz","title":"Define dormant mode daemon specification","description":"## Task
Specify the dormant mode daemon (ptd) that provides 24/7 monitoring with minimal overhead.

## Background
Section 3.7 specifies two operating modes:
- Active mode: Full collection + inference + plan (on-demand)
- Dormant mode (ptd): Lightweight monitoring with escalation

Dormant mode mechanics:
- Collect minimal signals at low frequency
- Use sketches/sparse localization for cheap monitoring
- Detect triggers (sustained load, PSI stall, orphan spikes)
- On trigger: quick scan → deep scan suspects → generate session → notify
- Never become the hog (strict overhead budget)

## Deliverables
- Trigger specification (what conditions escalate)
- Overhead budget (max CPU%, max memory)
- Collection cadence and signals
- Escalation protocol
- Cooldown and backoff rules
- Integration specifications:
  - Linux: systemd user service + timer
  - macOS: launchd agent
- Inbox UX for pending plans
- Coordination with manual/agent runs

## Technical Considerations
- Use EWMA + change detection for noise-robust triggers
- Consider time-uniform concentration bounds for sequential testing
- Must acquire pt lock before escalation
- nice/ionice for resource limiting
- Log triggers and escalations for debugging

## Safety Constraints
- Never take destructive actions from daemon
- Default: plan ready for review
- Optional: auto-apply non-destructive mitigations (pause/throttle) if configured

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.

 {"id":"process_triage-2l3","title":"EPIC: Phase 1 - Spec and Config Foundation","description":"## Overview
This epic covers all specification and configuration groundwork that must be completed before any implementation begins. This is the architectural foundation that everything else builds upon.

## Background & Context
The current pt tool is a simple bash script with basic heuristics. The transformation plan requires defining comprehensive schemas, contracts, and specifications that will guide all subsequent implementation. Without this foundation, implementation will lack coherence and the alien artifact quality will be compromised.

## Why This Matters
- **Consistency**: All subsequent phases need stable contracts to build against
- **Testability**: Schemas enable automated contract testing
- **Agent Integration**: AI agents need predictable, stable interfaces
- **Auditability**: Explicit specifications enable the galaxy-brain transparency goal
- **Safety**: Policy and guardrail definitions prevent ad-hoc safety implementations

## Scope
1. Package architecture (pt bash wrapper vs pt-core Rust monolith)
2. CLI surface design and stable output formats
3. Golden path UX definition
4. Session model and artifact directory layout
5. All JSON schemas (priors.json, policy.json, telemetry, capabilities)
6. Agent/robot contract definitions
7. Target identity and privilege contracts
8. Bundle/report specifications
9. Dormant daemon specifications
10. Galaxy-brain mode contract

## Success Criteria
- All schemas are defined in versioned JSON Schema files
- CLI surface is documented with stable option names and exit codes
- Contracts are testable (can write contract tests before implementation)
- No ambiguity remains about output formats or behavior modes

## Technical Considerations
- Use JSON Schema for all configuration schemas
- Schema versions must be explicit (semver)
- Exit codes must be automation-friendly (0=clean, 1=candidates exist, etc.)
- Consider backwards compatibility from the start

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.

 {"id":"process_triage-2pil","title":"Implement multi-objective kill set optimization","description":"## Overview
Implement goal-oriented / multi-objective **plan optimization** for selecting a set of actions that achieves user goals with minimal expected risk (Plan §5.14).

This bead is a deeper “optimizer” layer beneath the goal parser and per-candidate contribution estimation.

## Key Objectives
- Minimize total expected loss (primary)
- Achieve resource target(s): memory, CPU, ports, FD count, etc.
- Minimize number of destructive actions
- Respect safety constraints:
  - protected denylist
  - blast radius limits
  - FDR / alpha-investing budgets

## Formulation (Sketch)
Given candidates `i` with:
- expected loss `L_i(a)` for each allowed action `a`
- expected goal contribution `r_i(a)`

Solve (for a single scalar goal R):
```
minimize   Σ_i  L_i(a_i)
subject to Σ_i  r_i(a_i) ≥ R
           a_i ∈ allowed_actions(i)
```

For multiple goals, treat as:
- constrained multi-dimensional knapsack, or
- scalarize via weighted sum with user policy guidance.

## Algorithms (Practical)
- Greedy baseline: sort by (loss / contribution) with guardrails.
- DP exact for small N/targets.
- ILP/LP relaxation (optional) for larger sets.
- Local search to improve greedy solutions.

## UX / Output
- Provide the chosen plan plus 1–3 alternatives showing tradeoffs (Pareto frontier):
  - fewer actions / higher risk
  - more actions / more recovery

This must integrate into the normal plan output:
- `pt --goal ...` (human) and `pt agent plan --goal ...` (agent)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Produces a plan that meets goals when feasible and explains shortfall when infeasible.
- [ ] Respects hard safety constraints and never selects blocked actions.
- [ ] Produces alternative plans/tradeoff view when multiple solutions exist.

## Test Plan
- Unit: synthetic candidate sets with known optimal solutions.
- Unit: infeasible goals produce correct shortfall reporting.
- Integration: goal-aware plan generation uses optimizer output.

 {"id":"process_triage-ed3","title":"EPIC: Phase 11 - Pattern and Signature Library","description":"## Overview
Phase 11 builds a signature library for known process types with pre-calibrated priors and specialized detection logic.

## Background
The plan specifies bundled signatures for common processes: test runners, dev servers, build tools, databases, etc. Each signature includes characteristic patterns, expected lifetimes, typical resource usage, and calibrated Bayesian priors.

## Why It Matters
Generic priors work but specialized priors are much more accurate. A signature for 'jest test runner' knows that tests typically complete in minutes, not hours, and that high CPU during execution is normal. This domain knowledge dramatically improves inference accuracy.

## Phase Scope
1. Signature schema definition
2. Core signature library (50+ common processes)
3. Signature matching engine
4. Prior override system
5. Community signature format

## Signature Schema
- pattern: Regex matching command/exe
- category: test_runner|dev_server|build_tool|database|...
- expected_lifetime: {min, typical, max} in seconds
- resource_profile: {cpu: normal_range, memory: normal_range}
- priors: {abandoned: Beta(a,b), zombie: Beta(a,b), ...}
- detection_hints: Special signals for this process type
- kill_safety: {safe_to_term: bool, needs_drain: bool, ...}

## Bundled Signatures (Examples)
- Test runners: jest, pytest, bats, go test, cargo test
- Dev servers: next dev, vite, webpack-dev-server, rails s
- Build tools: make, cargo build, npm run build, gcc/clang
- Databases: postgres, mysql, redis, mongodb
- Agent shells: claude, codex, aider processes

## Matching Priority
1. Exact command match
2. Exe path match
3. Argument pattern match
4. Generic category fallback

## Success Criteria
- 50+ signatures for common dev tools
- Signature matching fast and accurate
- Priors improve inference accuracy (measured)
- Community contribution path documented","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:33:59.015590401Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T09:07:09.511765574Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T09:07:09.511765574Z","close_reason":"Superseded by process_triage-79x (canonical Phase 11 epic). Moved ed3.* child tasks under 79x.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-ed3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-2l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:42:57.285293183Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-ed3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-nao","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:42:57.990408611Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-ed3.1","title":"Implement user signature customization (signatures.json + add/remove/list)","description":"## Context
Phase 11 (Pattern/Signature Library).

## Problem
Users need to teach pt about their own tools and workflows. A curated library is not enough; we need first-class user customization.

## Requirements
- Config file: `~/.config/process_triage/signatures.json`
- Commands:
  - `pt signature list`
  - `pt signature add` (guided, validates pattern)
  - `pt signature remove`
- Merge behavior:
  - built-in signatures + user signatures; user overrides allowed
- Validation:
  - schema validation
  - test match preview (`--dry-run` match against current processes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User signatures load and merge deterministically.
- [ ] CLI commands work and are documented.
- [ ] Invalid signatures fail with actionable errors.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: merge/override behavior.
- E2E: create user signatures and confirm matches.

 {"id":"process_triage-ed3.2","title":"Implement signature-informed inference fast path (bypass/priors)","description":"## Context
Phase 11.

## Problem
Full inference can be expensive. When a process matches a high-confidence signature (e.g., known dev server pattern), we should be able to:
- skip deep inference
- or use signature-provided priors to accelerate and improve calibration

## Requirements
- Signature match returns a confidence score.
- If confidence ≥ threshold:
  - fast-path classification/prior override
  - still produce an evidence ledger (“matched signature X”)
- Track fast-path usage in telemetry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] High-confidence matches trigger fast-path behavior.
- [ ] Output remains explainable and auditable.
- [ ] Fast-path can be disabled via config/policy.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: match confidence thresholds.
- Golden tests: known signature fixtures.

-{"id":"process_triage-ed3.3","title":"Track and calibrate signature match confidence","description":"## Context
Phase 11.

## Problem
Signature confidence scores must be calibrated; otherwise we risk false certainty and unsafe recommendations.

## Approach
- Persist signature match outcomes:
  - match id, confidence score, eventual human decision/outcome
- Compute calibration metrics in shadow mode:
  - reliability curves for match confidence
  - confusion matrix by signature category
- Provide tooling to adjust thresholds and identify bad signatures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Telemetry contains sufficient fields to analyze signature performance.
- [ ] Produces a calibration report artifact (DuckDB query or HTML section).

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: aggregation and metric computation on fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria

## Test Plan

 {"id":"process_triage-erz","title":"Implement pt agent snapshot command","description":"## Overview
Implement the `pt agent snapshot` command that captures system state and creates a session for subsequent operations.

## From Plan Section 3.5

### Command Purpose
Returns a session_id and system state snapshot for subsequent agent operations. This is typically the first command in an agent workflow.

### Output Contract
```json
{
  'session_id': 'abc123',
  'host_id': 'devbox1',
  'timestamp': '2025-01-15T10:30:00Z',
  'system_state': {
    'load': [5.17, 4.89, 10.23],
    'cores': 64,
    'memory': {'total_gb': 499, 'used_gb': 281, 'available_gb': 218},
    'process_count': 412,
    'psi': {'cpu': 0.02, 'memory': 0.05, 'io': 0.01}
  },
  'capabilities': {
    'tools': {'perf': true, 'bpftrace': false, 'strace': true, ...},
    'permissions': {'can_sudo': false, 'can_ptrace': true}
  }
}
```

### Session Creation
- Generate unique session_id
- Create artifact directory: `~/.local/share/process_triage/sessions/<session_id>/`
- Persist initial snapshot

### Workflow Integration
```
Agent invocation 1: pt agent snapshot
  → Returns session_id, system state, capabilities
  → Agent decides: 'need to clean up, let me plan'

Agent invocation 2: pt agent plan --session <id>
  → Reuses session context from snapshot
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Command generates unique session_id
- [ ] System state captured accurately
- [ ] Capabilities detected and reported
- [ ] Session artifact directory created
- [ ] JSON output is well-formed
- [ ] --format json/md supported

## Dependencies
- Phase 1 (session model, CLI surface)

## Technical Notes
- System state collection should be fast (< 100ms)
- Capabilities detection uses cached results where available
- Session IDs should be short and URL-safe","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Dicklesworthstone","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:53:27.253564573Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T21:25:34.670323520Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-3ir","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:47:33.469249401Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-bwn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:12:15.331086567Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-qje","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:47:33.699867968Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-t6lf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:09:04.510934376Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-ew5y","title":"Implement pt agent apply command","description":"**Purpose**: Implement the `pt agent apply` command that executes actions from a plan.

**Plan Document Reference**: Section 3.5.1 - Agent CLI Contract

**CLI Surface**:
```
pt agent apply --session <id> [OPTIONS]
```

**Required Options**:
- `--session <id>` - Session containing the plan to execute
- `--recommended` - Apply all recommended actions from the plan
- `--pids 123,456` - Apply to specific PIDs (must exist in session plan)
- `--targets 123:<start_id>,456:<start_id>` - Explicit identity tuples (preferred)
- `--yes` - Required for execution (explicit confirmation)
- `--resume` - Resume an interrupted session from where it left off

**Confidence-Bounded Automation** (`--robot` controls):
- `--min-posterior <threshold>` - Only act if posterior > threshold (e.g., 0.99)
- `--max-blast-radius <amount>` - Limit total impact per run (e.g., "2GB", "5 processes")
- `--max-kills <N>` - Limit number of kill actions per run
- `--require-known-signature` - Only act on pattern library matches
- `--only-categories <list>` - Only act on specified categories
- `--exclude-categories <list>` - Never act on specified categories
- `--abort-on-unknown` - Stop if any unexpected condition encountered

**Safety Requirements**:
- MUST revalidate process identity immediately before applying any action
- If `(pid,start_id,uid)` mismatches, MUST block and require fresh plan
- MUST respect `--shadow` and `--dry-run` modes (no actual actions)
- MUST acquire per-user "pt lock" before executing
- MUST support supervisor-aware actions when supervisor detected

**Resumability**:
- Sessions track: plan, applied actions, pending actions, outcomes
- `--resume` continues from where interrupted
- Idempotent: re-running a completed action is a no-op

**Exit Codes**:
- 2: Actions executed successfully
- 3: Partial failure executing actions
- 4: Blocked by safety gates / policy
- 6: Session interrupted / resumable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the described scope with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).
- [ ] Includes unit/integration/E2E coverage for the highest-risk paths.

 {"id":"process_triage-f5o","title":"Implement progress event emission system","description":"## Task
Implement the progress event system that reports collection stages to TUI and agent CLI.

## Background
Both TUI and agent CLI need to show progress:
- scan_started, scan_progress, scan_complete
- deep_scan_started, deep_scan_progress, deep_scan_complete
- infer_started, infer_complete
- decide_started, decide_complete
- plan_ready

Events enable:
- TUI progress visualization
- pt agent tail --format jsonl streaming
- Timeout detection (no events = hung)

## Event Structure
{
  "event": "scan_progress",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T14:30:22Z",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "phase": "quick_scan",
  "progress": {"current": 150, "total": 312},
  "details": {"pids_scanned": 150}
}

## Implementation Notes
- Use channels for event dispatch
- Support multiple subscribers (TUI, file, network)
- Events should be cheap (not slow down collection)
- Include timing information for performance analysis

## Event Types
- session_started, session_ended
- quick_scan_{started,progress,complete}
- deep_scan_{started,progress,complete}
- inference_{started,progress,complete}
- decision_{started,complete}
- action_{started,complete,failed}
- plan_ready

## Deliverables
- Rust module: events/mod.rs
- Event type definitions
- Event emitter trait and implementations
- JSONL formatter for CLI
- Unit tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.
